flow_name: Univest – Variant 6: Dual CTA → Loss-Aversion Recover (6.1 → 6.2)

sample_quality.note: At n=50, completion rate has ±11.5% uncertainty at 90% confidence. Adequate for directional decisions; run 100+ for statistically robust sub-segment comparisons.

top_friction_points.friction: two-step structure adds friction versus single-screen variants
recovery screen 6.2 feels manipulative to high-skepticism users — 'they knew I'd hesitate'
loss-aversion framing on 6.2 ('don't miss out on today's trade') triggers urgency anxiety in privacy guardians
accuracy claim on 6.1 still without methodology
two different screens introduce pricing confusion — some assume 6.2 has higher cost

executive_summary: Univest – Variant 6: Dual CTA → Loss-Aversion Recover (6.1 → 6.2) achieved a 44.0% completion rate across all 50 personas (mid-tier of the six variants tested). The two-step structure adds friction but enables targeted messaging at each step. Core non-converters are Skeptics (0.0% completion), Privacy Guardians (0.0%), and the Junk cohort (0%). Enthusiasts convert at 93.3% across all variants.

usability_findings.screen: 6_2 - Loss-Aversion Recovery – '₹1 is all it takes' (6.2)
usability_findings.finding: Auto-renewal at ₹2,799 in 9px footer text is the single largest drop-off driver, perceived as a subscription trap by Skeptics and Privacy Guardians.
usability_findings.evidence: Pay ₹1 but renews at ₹2,799? That's a subscription trap. I know 'cancel anytime' often means nothing.
usability_findings.recommendation: Increase auto-renewal disclosure to prominent position. Add explicit cancel-confirmation flow during onboarding.

usability_findings.finding: The 84.7% accuracy claim is the most-cited friction point across all archetypes. Without a methodology link it reads as an invented marketing number.
usability_findings.evidence: Why is it exactly 84.7%? That specificity without evidence is a classic sign of fabricated social proof.
usability_findings.recommendation: Replace with a 'Verified 3-year performance summary' with a link to historical call data.

segment_analysis.summary: Enthusiasts lead conversion (93.3%). Skeptics and Privacy Guardians anchor the bottom (0.0%). Pragmatists are the swing segment — most sensitive to trust-signal quality and pricing transparency.
segment_analysis.high_propensity_segment: The Enthusiast (Male/Female, 23-31, ₹22k-₹75k). Low friction threshold, impulsive ₹1 tap, FOMO-driven.
segment_analysis.low_propensity_segment: The Skeptic and Privacy Guardian (Mixed, 28-41). Systematic trust evaluation, dark-pattern detection, household financial accountability.
